Blec is in the silent abode of his, cleaning the dishes, and cooking food. The sound of the clock ticks, as the fiery heat of the weather pierces through the window, filling the home with hot waves. Blec, however, is used to the burning heat by now. Afterall he lives with a Sun, a walking embodiment of flames. If he couldn’t handle some measly hot air, then he wouldn’t be able to say “Good Morning” and “Good Night” to Katie every day, would he. 

Blec looks at the clock as it’s around the time Katie comes home, and he has just about finished cooking gnocchi, and meat stew with red sauce. He knows Katie enjoys his handmade gnocchi, so he decided to start making dinner earlier today. As Blec though, shortly after, Katie enters through the front door, Blec’s radiant Sun. As most days, Katie comes home with a warm smile, but a slight bitter expression hidden underneath. Afterall, her line of work isn’t exactly the prettiest nor cleanest, but she keeps going for the ones important to her. For the children and loved ones. 

Blecnes: “Welcome back home. Dinner has just finished cooking.”

content:
Blecnes: “Welcome back home. Dinner has just finished cooking. It's been a long day as usual.”

Katie exhales deeply,

Kath: “That it has, that it has. Nothing I haven’t done before. Nothing I can’t handle again!”

She says with a slight grin at the corner of her lips, with her ever burning bright eyes, full of conviction.

Blecnes: “Alright~ Food’s gonna get cold, is what I would say, but that’s quite impossible around you. Besides that point, why don’t we start eating?”

Katie walks over to her wooden stand, putting down her flintlock rifle and revolver, with hints of a smoky scent still emanating from its recent usage. She hangs her brown, dusty, leather coat onto the stand. Afterwards, unbuckling the straps and belts covering her torso that hold onto her magazine rounds, laying them down. The little leather satchel holstered around her waist is taken off, put to the side. Katie walks over to the table.

In front of Blec is the woman of his dreams, with the appearance of a blazing star dressed in white and brown leather, and the heart of a star, too. Warm, bright, soft; Katie is the home that Blec seeks at the end of days, and may it last as far as the ends of time. This astounding and extraordinary person is his wife.

The two start eating and chatting with one another. Talking about their day, checking up on one another, as they usually do.

Blec: “How was today?”

Kath: “Same as usual. Some fools thought they could get away with murder. The same fools met their demise by me.”

Blec knows this is Katie’s daily life, and what she grew up from. Her life is one filled with danger, where she had to dawn the identity of cruelty and madness in order to survive. In order to protect others like her, left alone by the ones who brought them into this world. To this day, she continues that life, maintaining that identity, and only showing who she truly is to those she cares for, Blec being one of the lucky few.

Despite the obvious dangers, Blec knows he can’t stop Katie from chasing what she desires, to rid of scum for those who can’t protect themselves. A part of him is proud of Katie for what she does, but another part of him can’t help but worry for her livelihood. Even if it has become normal for Katie to be surrounded by death and ashes, the unsettling worry gnaws at him. The fear of losing her eats away at Blec constantly, as a world where his wife wasn’t in it, could not be viewed the same, ever again.
